attractions.name: Arkansas River Trail Arkansas River Trail Official Site, City of Little Rock
attractions.category: Park / Recreational Trail Little Rock Convention & Visitors Bureau, City of Little Rock
attractions.description: The Arkansas River Trail is a premier multi-use trail system stretching over 15 miles along both banks of the Arkansas River in Central Arkansas. It serves as a central hub for outdoor recreation, connecting multiple city parks, museums, and residential areas via several iconic pedestrian bridges. Arkansas River Trail Official Site, City of Little Rock
attractions.address: 400 President Clinton Ave, Little Rock, AR 72201-1638 Tripadvisor, Little Rock Convention & Visitors Bureau
attractions.city: Little Rock City of Little Rock
attractions.region: River Market / Downtown Little Rock / North Little Rock Little Rock Convention & Visitors Bureau, Rockwater Village

attractions.days_open: Monday - Sunday City of North Little Rock, Airial Travel

Clinton Presidential Park Bridge Arkansas River Trail Official Site
Two Rivers Park Bridge Arkansas River Trail Official Site
Vogel Schwartz Sculpture Garden Little Rock Convention & Visitors Bureau
Scenic views of the Little Rock and North Little Rock skylines Arkansas River Trail Official Site
attractions.tips: Bring plenty of water and sunscreen as large sections are unshaded. Trail users should keep to the right and call out warnings when passing. Early morning visits are recommended to avoid midday heat and enjoy wildlife viewing. City of North Little Rock, Reddit

attractions.name: Arkansas State Capitol
attractions.description: The Arkansas State Capitol is the seat of the state's government and houses the Arkansas General Assembly. A monumental neoclassical structure built between 1899 and 1915, it sits atop Capitol Hill and is a prominent feature of the Little Rock skyline (Wikipedia, Encyclopedia of Arkansas).
attractions.hours: Weekdays: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Weekends: 10:00 AM - 3:00 PM; State Holidays: 10:00 AM - 3:00 PM (Arkansas Secretary of State)
attractions.admission: Free (Arkansas Secretary of State)
highlights: Exterior constructed from Batesville limestone and Yule marble (Wikipedia)
Front entrance doors made of solid bronze by Tiffany & Company, standing 10 feet tall (Arkansas Secretary of State)
Central dome covered in 24-karat gold leaf (Encyclopedia of Arkansas)
A 4,000-pound, 12-foot diameter chandelier hanging in the Rotunda (TravelGumbo)
Historic Old Supreme Court room and Governor’s Reception Room (Arkansas Secretary of State)
attractions.tips: Self-guided tour books are available at the rotunda desk for those visiting on weekends when guided tours are not offered (Arkansas Secretary of State). Guided tours should be scheduled in advance via email. Free visitor parking is located around the Capitol complex (TravelGumbo).
attractions.nearby: Old State House Museum, William J. Clinton Presidential Library, Little Rock River Market, Museum of Discovery, Little Rock Central High School National Historic Site (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Doe's Eat Place
attractions.description: Doe's Eat Place is a nationally renowned Little Rock landmark known for its no-frills Southern atmosphere and award-winning family-style steaks. Originally inspired by a joint in the Mississippi Delta, it gained national prominence as a favorite hangout for Bill Clinton's campaign staff during the 1992 presidential election.
attractions.hours: Lunch: Monday-Friday 11:00 AM - 2:00 PM; Dinner: Monday-Saturday 5:00 PM - 9:00 PM
attractions.admission: Free entry; menu items range from approximately $8.25 for appetizers to $52.00+ for large family-style steaks ($30-$35 per pound).
highlights: Famous for enormous family-style steaks sold by the pound (T-bone, Porterhouse, and Sirloin).
Signature hot tamales served with homemade chili.
Historical connection to President Bill Clinton and his 1992 campaign staff.
No-frills atmosphere featuring walls covered in photographs and political memorabilia.
Menu features hand-cut fries, double cheeseburgers, and broiled or fried shrimp.
Inducted into the Arkansas Food Hall of Fame.
attractions.tips: Reservations are recommended and accepted for groups of seven or more. Steaks are served family-style and are quite large, often suitable for sharing among multiple people. The atmosphere is casual, so feel free to wear jeans, and be aware that 'well-done' steaks are discouraged and not guaranteed.
attractions.nearby: Arkansas State Capitol, Old State House Museum, Riverfront Park, Little Rock Central High School National Historic Site

attractions.name: First Security Amphitheater (formerly Riverfest Amphitheater)
attractions.description: First Security Amphitheater is Central Arkansas' premier outdoor concert venue, featuring a stunning stage set against the backdrop of the Arkansas River and illuminated historic bridges. Originally known as Riverfest Amphitheater, this iconic 7-acre site hosts major touring artists, local festivals, and annual community celebrations within the heart of downtown Little Rock's River Market District. Little Rock Convention & Visitors Bureau, First Security Amphitheater Info
attractions.region: River Market District / Downtown Little Rock Convention & Visitors Bureau
attractions.hours: Park Hours: Daily 6:00 AM – 10:00 PM; Box Office: Typically opens at 5:00 PM on event days only Ticketmaster, She Studios
attractions.days_open: Open daily (as part of Riverfront Park); events scheduled primarily on weekends and selected weekdays City of Little Rock
attractions.admission: Varies by event (typically $25-$150+ for major concerts); many community events like 'Pops on the River' offer free general admission with optional reserved seating (e.g., $15) Ticketmaster, Little Rock Convention & Visitors Bureau
highlights: 7,000+ capacity with a mix of fixed seating, pit areas, and a spacious lawn Little Rock Convention & Visitors Bureau
Stunning riverfront views featuring the illuminated Junction Bridge Airial Travel
Hosts iconic annual events including Pops on the River and Jazz in the Park Little Rock Convention & Visitors Bureau
State-of-the-art 60'x60' outdoor stage designed for major touring productions Little Rock Convention & Visitors Bureau
Located within the 33-acre Julius Breckling Riverfront Park Little Rock Convention & Visitors Bureau
attractions.tips: Arrive early to secure prime spots on the lawn; low-back lawn chairs and blankets are encouraged for most shows. For free parking, consider parking near the Clinton Library and taking a scenic stroll along the riverfront to the venue. Airial Travel
attractions.nearby: River Market District, William J. Clinton Presidential Library and Museum, Museum of Discovery, Junction Bridge, Arkansas River Trail Wikipedia)

attractions.name: Junction Bridge
attractions.description: Originally constructed in 1884 as a railroad bridge, the Junction Bridge was converted into a pedestrian and bicycle bridge in 2008. It serves as a key link in the Arkansas River Trail, connecting downtown Little Rock with North Little Rock, and features a iconic lift span that is illuminated nightly with thousands of LED lights. Little Rock Convention & Visitors Bureau, Encyclopedia of Arkansas
attractions.region: River Market District / Downtown Little Rock Convention & Visitors Bureau
attractions.hours: 6:00 AM - 10:00 PM Daily Aroundus, Yelp
attractions.admission: Free Tripadvisor
highlights: Part of the 15-mile Arkansas River Trail loop system Encyclopedia of Arkansas
Features a 360-foot lift span permanently fixed 38 feet above the river Arkansas.com
The south end of the bridge rests on the original 'little rock' geological formation that named the city Encyclopedia of Arkansas
Illuminated nightly with thousands of LED lights as part of the 'River Lights in the Rock' installation Little Rock Convention & Visitors Bureau
Offers panoramic skyline views of Little Rock and North Little Rock Yelp

attractions.tips: Visit at night to experience the colorful LED light display. Note that the elevators are occasionally out of order, so be prepared to climb 58 stairs if needed. Look for the 'postcard frame' near the bridge for a unique photo opportunity. Tripadvisor, Yelp

attractions.name: Little Rock Mural Tour
attractions.category: Landmark, Entertainment Little Rock Convention & Visitors Bureau
attractions.description: The Little Rock Mural Tour showcases a thriving arts scene that extends beyond traditional galleries and onto the city's walls and doors. This vibrant collection features larger-than-life critters, inspirational abstracts, and poignant social justice art that brings the city to life with colorful scenes of unity, nature, and history. Little Rock Convention & Visitors Bureau

attractions.name: Mosaic Templars Cultural Center
attractions.description: The Mosaic Templars Cultural Center is a state-funded museum dedicated to preserving and interpreting the African American experience in Arkansas from 1870 to the present. Housed in a state-of-the-art facility on the site of the original Mosaic Templars of America headquarters, it serves as a center for education, cultural preservation, and community engagement. Arkansas Heritage
attractions.region: West Ninth Street District / Downtown Little Rock
attractions.hours: Tuesday – Saturday: 9 a.m. – 5 p.m. Arkansas Heritage
attractions.admission: Free (Galleries: Free, Grounds: Free) Arkansas Heritage

Immersive and interactive permanent galleries showcasing Arkansas’s African American history Arkansas Heritage
A 360-degree theater that provides visitors with an engaging multimedia experience Discover Arkansas
The "A City Within A City" exhibit exploring the history of the West 9th Street Business District Tiedye Travels
Accredited by the American Alliance of Museums, one of the few Black history institutions in the South to hold this honor Arkansas Heritage
attractions.tips: Self-guided tours are free and require no reservation; however, guided tours for groups of 10 or more should be requested at least one week in advance. Free museum parking is available for visitors. Arkansas Heritage

attractions.name: Old State House Museum Arkansas Heritage
attractions.category: Museum Little Rock Convention & Visitors Bureau
attractions.description: The Old State House Museum is the original state capitol building of Arkansas and the oldest standing state capitol building west of the Mississippi River. Since its construction began in 1833, the building has witnessed pivotal historical events, including Arkansas's admission to the Union and President Bill Clinton’s election night victory speeches. Arkansas Heritage, Little Rock Convention & Visitors Bureau
attractions.address: 300 W. Markham Street, Little Rock, AR 72201 Arkansas Heritage
attractions.region: Downtown Little Rock Convention & Visitors Bureau

attractions.tips: Guided tours are approximately 50 minutes long and are offered every hour on the hour, with the last tour beginning at 4 p.m. Plan to spend at least one hour to explore the various permanent collections, especially the First Ladies' gowns. Little Rock Guest Guide

attractions.name: Philander Smith University
attractions.description: Founded in 1877, Philander Smith University is a private, historically Black, four-year liberal arts institution affiliated with the United Methodist Church and the United Negro College Fund (UNCF). It is Little Rock’s oldest historically black educational institution and serves as a pioneer in social justice activism. (Philander Smith University, Wikipedia)
attractions.address: 900 W. Daisy L Gatson Bates Dr.
attractions.hours: Monday - Thursday: 8:00 AM - 6:00 PM (Summer Hours); Regular hours typically Monday - Friday: 8:30 AM - 5:00 PM (Philander Smith University)
attractions.days_open: Monday - Friday (Closed Friday in Summer) (Philander Smith University)
attractions.admission: Free for campus tours (advance scheduling required) (Philander Smith University)
highlights: Oldest historically Black educational institution in Little Rock (Little Rock CVB)
Member of the United Negro College Fund (UNCF) (Wikipedia)
Accredited by the Higher Learning Commission (Philander Smith University)
Pioneer in Civil Rights activism and nonviolent resistance (Wikipedia)
Recently transitioned from College to University status in 2023 (Wikipedia)
Features the Panther mascot with green and gold colors (Philander Smith University)
attractions.tips: Prospective students should schedule tours in advance by calling 501-370-5221 or emailing admissions@philander.edu. The campus is located in the historic Quapaw Quarter, offering a rich cultural and architectural backdrop for visitors. (Philander Smith University)
attractions.nearby: Quapaw Quarter Historic District, Little Rock Central High School National Historic Site, MacArthur Park, Arkansas Museum of Fine Arts (Little Rock CVB, Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Quapaw Quarter Historic District
attractions.description: The Quapaw Quarter is a nine-square-mile historic section of Little Rock that encompasses some of the city's oldest and most significant residential and business neighborhoods. It features a diverse collection of architectural styles, including Greek Revival, Queen Anne, and Victorian homes, many of which are listed on the National Register of Historic Places Wikipedia, Yahoo Lifestyle.
attractions.hours: Monday-Saturday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Sunday: 10:00 AM - 3:00 PM (April-October) Quapaw Quarter Association
attractions.days_open: Daily (Visitor Center has seasonal Sunday hours) Quapaw Quarter Association
attractions.admission: Free for general district exploration and visiting Curran Hall; Annual Spring Tour of Homes: $30-$40 At Home in Arkansas, Airial Travel

Arkansas Governor's Mansion
MacArthur Park Historic District and Arsenal Tower
Villa Marre (featured in 'Designing Women')
Mount Holly Cemetery ('Westminster Abbey of Arkansas')
South Main Residential Historic District (SoMa)
attractions.tips: Start your visit at Curran Hall to pick up a map and brochures for self-guided walking or driving tours. Most historic homes are private residences and can only be viewed from the street, except during the annual Spring Tour of Homes Little Rock CVB, Tripadvisor.

attractions.name: River Market District
attractions.description: The River Market District is the vibrant heart of downtown Little Rock, serving as the city's unofficial cultural center and premier entertainment neighborhood. It features the Ottenheimer Market Hall with diverse food vendors, scenic riverfront views, and is the cornerstone of the city's revitalized downtown scene.
attractions.hours: Ottenheimer Market Hall: Monday - Saturday 7:00 AM - 6:00 PM (Individual vendor hours vary); Farmers Market (Seasonal): Saturdays 7:00 AM - 1:00 PM; Entertainment District: Open daily, bars/restaurants typically open until late.
attractions.days_open: Monday - Saturday (Ottenheimer Market Hall); Saturday (Farmers Market, seasonal)
attractions.admission: Free (Public areas and Market Hall); Special events and specific museums (e.g., Museum of Discovery, Clinton Library) may have separate fees.
highlights: Ottenheimer Market Hall with 17+ international food vendors
Seasonal Little Rock Farmers Market (Saturdays, May-August)
Vogel Schwartz Sculpture Garden in Riverfront Park
First Security Amphitheater for outdoor concerts and events
METRO Streetcar guided tours through the district
Proximity to the Clinton Presidential Library and Museum of Discovery
attractions.tips: Visit on Saturday mornings from May to August to experience the full Farmers Market. Free parking is available for Farmers Market patrons in the River Market Parking Deck at 500 E. 2nd St. Use the METRO Streetcar for a historic tour of the district.
attractions.nearby: Clinton Presidential Library, Museum of Discovery, Riverfront Park, Arkansas River Trail, Junction Bridge, Old State House Museum.

attractions.name: Robert A. Young Federal Building
attractions.description: The Robert A. Young Federal Building is a massive 20-story Art Deco office complex originally built in 1933 as a railroad merchandise mart and warehouse. Today, it serves as a major hub for federal operations, housing over 30 government agencies and approximately 3,000 employees. The building is listed on the National Register of Historic Places and is renowned for its architectural and historical significance. U.S. General Services Administration, McCarthy Building Companies
attractions.hours: Monday: 6:00 AM - 6:00 PM; Tuesday: 6:00 AM - 6:00 PM; Wednesday: 6:00 AM - 6:00 PM; Thursday: 6:00 AM - 6:00 PM; Friday: 6:00 AM - 6:00 PM; Saturday: Closed; Sunday: Closed Yelp
attractions.admission: Free U.S. General Services Administration
highlights: Listed on the National Register of Historic Places (NRHP) for its architectural significance.
Iconic Art Deco design featuring stepped vertical pilasters and terracotta ornamentation.
Houses over 30 federal agencies, including the IRS, USCIS, and U.S. Army Corps of Engineers.
Originally served as the St. Louis Mart and Terminal Warehouse, a symbol of the city's railway power.
Features a grand historic lobby with marble finishes and an ornate coffered ceiling.
Completed a $75 million seismic renovation in 2019 to ensure stability in the region.
attractions.tips: Visitors must present a valid government-issued photo ID and undergo security screening to enter. Weapons and other prohibited items are strictly forbidden. Public parking is available in nearby surface lots and garages along Spruce Street. A cafeteria and convenience shops are located on the first floor for public use during business hours. Reddit, Deployment Psychology
attractions.nearby: Gateway Arch, Busch Stadium, Old Courthouse, National Blues Museum, Soldiers Memorial Military Museum Explore St. Louis

attractions.description: The Capital Hotel has been the "Grand Dame" of downtown Little Rock for 150 years, known for its Gilded Age elegance and status as the city's "front porch." This historic landmark offers a blend of timeless Southern style and modern luxury, having hosted numerous political leaders and celebrities including President Ulysses S. Grant. The Capital Hotel Official Website, Encyclopedia of Arkansas

The restaurant offers a wide variety of fresh fried and grilled seafood, including catfish, shrimp, oysters, and crawfish, in a family-friendly atmosphere decorated with a unique collection of 'singing' Billy Bass fish.

attractions.hours: Sunday - Thursday: 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM; Friday - Saturday: 11:00 AM - 10:00 PM

attractions.name: Historic Arkansas Museum
attractions.description: The Historic Arkansas Museum is a regional history and specialty art museum that encompasses a historic city block featuring several of Little Rock's oldest buildings. It showcases Arkansas's heritage through a restored 1850s farmstead, extensive galleries of Arkansas-made art, and exhibits detailing the history of indigenous peoples and the state's frontier era. Arkansas Heritage, Encyclopedia of Arkansas
attractions.address: 200 E. Third Street, Little Rock, AR 72201
attractions.hours: Tuesday – Saturday: 9 a.m. – 5 p.m.; Sunday: 1 p.m. – 5 p.m.; Closed Mondays and major holidays (New Year’s Day, Easter, Thanksgiving, Christmas Eve, and Christmas Day). Arkansas Heritage
attractions.admission: Galleries: Free; Grounds Tours: $2.50 for adults, $1.50 for seniors (65+), $1.00 for children (under 18), Free for Museum members. Arkansas Heritage
highlights: Hinderliter Grog Shop (Little Rock's oldest standing building, built in 1826)
The 1850s Farmstead with period outbuildings

The J.M. Black Bowie Knife Gallery
Living Craft Workshops including chair caning and blacksmithing demonstrations
Native American and Trail of Tears history exhibits
attractions.tips: Free parking is provided for visitors in the museum's dedicated lot. While the indoor galleries are free, it is highly recommended to pay the small fee for a guided grounds tour to gain access and historical context for the restored buildings. Allow approximately 1 to 2 hours to explore both the exhibits and the historic block. Tripadvisor

attractions.description: Julius Breckling Riverfront Park is a 33-acre urban oasis stretching 11 blocks along the south bank of the Arkansas River in downtown Little Rock. It serves as the city's 'front lawn,' offering scenic river views, recreational trails, and over 100 public art installations. (Little Rock Convention & Visitors Bureau, Evendo)

It features a blend of wild nature trails and developed recreational facilities, including sports courts and a modern playground, providing a tranquil retreat close to the city center (Kupi.com). The park is split into north and south sectors, offering a variety of terrain from paved paths to rugged mountain biking trails (Little Rock Convention & Visitors Bureau).

attractions.hours: 08:00 AM - 10:00 PM (City of Little Rock Pavilion Reservation Guidelines)

Recreational facilities including tennis courts, pickleball courts, and baseball/softball fields (Kupi.com)
Modern children's playground with varied equipment and a sand surface (Instead of the Dishes)

Historical significance, with a master plan originally commissioned in 1931 from landscape architect John Nolen (City of Little Rock)
attractions.tips: The trails can be rocky and technical, so mountain bikers should exercise caution; additionally, parking at the main Cedar Hill Road entrance can be busy on weekends (Kupi.com, Little Rock Convention & Visitors Bureau). Visitors should also be aware that the park is split into separate north and south loops, requiring a short trip through the neighborhood via Beechwood Street to connect them (Little Rock Convention & Visitors Bureau).

attractions.description: Pinnacle Mountain State Park stands as a geographically diverse centerpiece just west of Little Rock, featuring the iconic 1,011-foot cone-shaped Pinnacle Mountain. This day-use park is dedicated to environmental education, recreation, and preservation, offering over 33 miles of hiking trails and 19 miles of mountain bike paths across 2,356 acres of forest, wetlands, and rivers. (Arkansas State Parks, Little Rock CVB)
attractions.hours: Visitor Center: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM Daily (Closed Thanksgiving, Christmas Eve, and Christmas Day); Park Gates/Day Use: 6:30 AM to one hour after sunset (Arkansas State Parks)

Paddle the Big and Little Maumelle Rivers with on-site kayak and paddleboard rentals available at self-serve kiosks. (Little Rock CVB)

attractions.tips: Bring plenty of water and wear sturdy hiking boots for the rocky summit trails; the West Summit is generally easier than the East Summit. Arrive early on weekends as parking areas can fill up quickly. Visit the Loblolly Outpost near the visitor center for handcrafted ice cream after your hike. (YouTube, Little Rock CVB)

attractions.name: Emerald Park
attractions.description: Emerald Park is a 103-acre linear park perched atop the Big Rock cliffs, offering some of the most spectacular views in Central Arkansas. The park features paved and natural trails that overlook the Arkansas River, downtown Little Rock, and the State Capitol. It serves as a scenic destination for hikers, cyclists, and nature lovers seeking panoramic vistas and tranquil wooded paths. (City of North Little Rock, Arkansas.com)

attractions.name: Lake Maumelle
attractions.description: Lake Maumelle is a scenic 8,900-acre man-made reservoir located just northwest of Little Rock that serves as the primary drinking water source for the metropolitan area. Surrounded by the picturesque Ouachita Mountains, the lake features a 70-mile shoreline and is a premier destination for sailing regattas, premier fishing, and nature hiking. While activities like swimming and water skiing are prohibited to maintain water quality, the area offers a peaceful retreat for outdoor enthusiasts and wildlife watchers. Central Arkansas Water, Little Rock CVB
attractions.hours: Monday: Sunrise - Sunset; Tuesday: Sunrise - Sunset; Wednesday: Sunrise - Sunset; Thursday: Sunrise - Sunset; Friday: Sunrise - Sunset; Saturday: Sunrise - Sunset; Sunday: Sunrise - Sunset Central Arkansas Water

Note that kayaks and canoes are not permitted beyond the Highway 10 bridge for safety and conservation reasons. Central Arkansas Water

attractions.name: Little Rock Central High School National Historic Site
attractions.description: Little Rock Central High School was the epicenter of the 1957 desegregation crisis and serves as a fundamental test for the United States to enforce civil rights following Brown v. Board of Education. It is uniquely distinguished as the only operating high school in the nation located within the boundaries of a National Historic Site. The site commemorates the bravery of the Little Rock Nine and offers a deep look into the struggle for racial equality in American education (National Park Service, Civil Rights Trail).
attractions.address: 2120 W. Daisy L. Gatson Bates Drive, Little Rock, AR 72202-5212
attractions.region: Central Little Rock / Southwest of Downtown (TCLF)
attractions.hours: Tuesday - Saturday: 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM (Regular); Tuesday - Saturday: 9:00 AM - 4:30 PM (Summer, June 1 - August 31); Closed Sundays and Mondays (National Park Service)
attractions.admission: Free (National Park Service)
highlights: Visitor Center featuring multimedia exhibits and interpretive films on the desegregation crisis (Wikipedia)
Commemorative Garden with nine trees and benches honoring the Little Rock Nine (TCLF)
Historic Mobil Gas Station that served as the media headquarters in 1957 (National Park Service)
Ranger-led programs providing guided tours of the school grounds (National Park Service)
The "United" and "Transcendence" sculptures symbolizing social change and equality (National Park Service)
Neo-Gothic and Art Deco architecture of the massive 1927 school building (Encyclopedia of Arkansas)
attractions.tips: Visitors should start at the Visitor Center for maps and background information. Ranger-led tours are highly recommended but must be reserved at least 24 hours in advance as space is limited. Since the high school is a functioning educational facility, public access to the interior is restricted to these guided tours. Parking is free in the lot adjacent to the visitor center (National Park Service, Yelp).

The creamery is a Certified Green Restaurant that collaborates with local farms and businesses to create unique, community-focused flavors.

attractions.name: Petit Jean State Park
attractions.description: Petit Jean State Park is an Arkansas classic, a natural and historic treasure that was the state's first state park. Established in 1923, it spans over 2,700 acres atop Petit Jean Mountain, featuring rugged beauty, ancient geology, and rustic stone and log facilities built by the Civilian Conservation Corps.

Restaurant: Peak Season (Mar-Nov) 7:00 a.m. - 9:00 p.m.; Off-Peak (Dec-Feb) 8:00 a.m. - 8:00 p.m. Public Pool (Memorial Day-Labor Day): Wed-Sun 11:00 a.m. - 6:00 p.m. Boathouse (Memorial Day-Labor Day): Wed-Sun 8:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. Museum of Automobiles: Open daily 10:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m.

Cell service is available on park roads but may be spotty on trails; download offline maps beforehand. Pets are welcome but must be kept on a leash, and shot records are required for cabin stays. Book camping or lodge stays well in advance for peak seasons.

attractions.name: Plum Bayou Mounds Archeological State Park (formerly Toltec Mounds Archeological State Park)

The site features eighteen earthen mounds and two large plazas constructed by the Plum Bayou culture between A.D. 650 and 1050, aligned to track astronomical events like solstices and equinoxes. Named after prominent businessman Charles Stifft, the neighborhood is renowned for its diverse collection of historic Craftsman bungalows and its role as a major hub on the former Pulaski Heights trolley line. It remains a vibrant mixed-use area featuring charming residential streets alongside a robust commercial strip on West Markham Street. (Wikipedia, Encyclopedia of Arkansas)

West Markham Commercial Strip: A historic band of storefronts featuring local favorites like the Oyster Bar and quirky gift shops (Bang-Up Betty).
Woodruff Community Garden: A neighborhood-led green space and mural site located on the southern edge of the district (CVSSNA).
attractions.tips: Explore the neighborhood on foot to best appreciate the historic architecture and local murals. Many local shops offer rear parking for easier access during busy times on West Markham Street. (CVSSNA, Bang-Up Betty)

attractions.name: Vino's Brew Pub (also known as Vino's Pizza-Pub-Brewery)
attractions.description: Vino's is Little Rock's original brewpub and a cultural staple established in 1990. It is known for serving hand-tossed, stone-baked New York-style pizza and award-winning craft beers brewed on-site, while also functioning as a prominent venue for live music and punk rock performances. (Vino's Brew Pub), (Encyclopedia of Arkansas)
attractions.hours: Monday - Wednesday: 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM; Thursday - Sunday: 11:00 AM - 10:00 PM (Late night hours until 12:00 AM on Friday/Saturday reported by some sources). (Vino's Brew Pub), (Tripadvisor)
attractions.admission: Free admission for the restaurant; live shows typically range from $10 to $15 depending on the event. (Vino's Brew Pub), (Tripadvisor)
highlights: Little Rock's oldest and original brewpub, established in 1990. (Vino's Brew Pub)

Offers a non-smoking environment with outdoor seating available. (Yelp)
attractions.tips: Visit on Monday for $2 off calzones or Tuesday for $3 off whole pizzas. There is plenty of free parking available in the lot across the street. (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: War Memorial Park
attractions.description: War Memorial Park is a treasured 117-acre urban green space located in the heart of Little Rock, characterized by its rolling terrain and the scenic Coleman Creek. The park serves as a major community hub for recreation and gathering, featuring a blend of natural beauty and modern amenities following its transformation from the original Arkansas State Fairgrounds. City of Little Rock, Encyclopedia of Arkansas

attractions.name: War Memorial Stadium Official Site
attractions.description: War Memorial Stadium is a historic multi-purpose venue in Little Rock that has hosted major college football games, concerts, and events for over 70 years Official Site. Dedicated as a tribute to Arkansans who gave their lives in service during the two World Wars, it serves as a secondary home for the University of Arkansas Razorbacks and hosts the state's high school football championships Wikipedia).
attractions.hours: Closed to the general public except during scheduled events; available for rent 7 days a week Official Site
attractions.days_open: Available for events 7 days a week Official Site
attractions.admission: Ticket prices vary by event and are sold at outlets designated by each event's promoter Official Site. The stadium and its memorial plaza are generally free to view from the exterior when no events are scheduled Tripadvisor.
highlights: Dedicated as a living tribute to Arkansans who served in World War I and II Arkansas Department of Parks, Heritage and Tourism
Secondary home stadium for the University of Arkansas Razorbacks football team Wikipedia)
Home of the Arkansas Activities Association high school football state championship games Official Site
Features the Sturgis Veterans Plaza, a memorial circle with a granite tribute to fallen soldiers Official Site
Home field for the Little Rock Rangers soccer club Little Rock Convention & Visitors Bureau
Has hosted legendary musical acts like The Rolling Stones, Billy Joel, and Elton John Wikipedia)
attractions.tips: Check the official event calendar for specific parking and tailgating policies, as they vary by event Official Site. Note that the stadium is a no-smoking facility and generally does not allow re-entry once you have entered an event Official Site.

attractions.name: Welcome Home Little Rock Mural
attractions.description: The "Welcome Home Little Rock" mural (often referred to as the Sweet Home mural due to its welcoming theme) is a landmark public art piece created in 2011. It features a realistic depiction of a local family alongside the city's "501" area code and incorporates 3D elements like actual bicycles mounted to the wall.
attractions.address: 600 Scott Street (on the Best Park Parking Deck)
attractions.hours: Open 24/7 (Outdoor Mural)
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: Prominently features the city's '501' area code
Depicts a realistic and heartwarming scene of a local family
Includes interactive 3D elements with real bicycles attached to the wall
Located in the heart of Baker's Alley, a hub for downtown street art
A signature 'selfie' spot that welcomes visitors to the downtown district
attractions.tips: The mural is located in an alleyway, so visit during daylight hours for the best lighting and photography; park in the adjacent 6th & Scott parking deck for easy access.

attractions.name: Winrock Farms, Inc. Morrilton Chamber of Commerce
attractions.category: Agricultural Enterprise / Cattle Ranch / Landmark Encyclopedia of Arkansas
attractions.description: Winrock Farms is a world-renowned cattle operation founded in 1953 by Winthrop Rockefeller, who later served as Governor of Arkansas. Located atop Petit Jean Mountain, it became a model for innovative agricultural practices and the birthplace of the state's purebred Santa Gertrudis and Red Angus cattle industries. Today, it remains a family-owned operation and home to the Winthrop Rockefeller Institute, continuing its legacy of stewardship and rural development Encyclopedia of Arkansas, Winthrop Rockefeller Institute.
